pr.title: ft: decompile Kirby & Ice Climbers special moves
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled a batch of fighter special-move functions for Kirby copy abilities and Ice Climbers, replacing placeholder /// # stubs with matching C implementations. The PR body says this covered 5 functions in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c for Kirby copying Ness neutral-B, 2 functions in ftKb_SpecialNZd.c for Kirby copying Sheik/Zelda-related neutral-B handling, 5 functions in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c for Kirby copying Pikachu/Pichu and Bowser/Giga Bowser neutral-B behavior, and 5 functions in ftPp_SpecialS.c for Ice Climbers up-B and down-B. It also added needed prototypes and refined Ice Climber attribute struct fields. Verification reported all functions at 100% objdiff match and main.dol SHA1 OK.
what_changed: Implemented Kirby Ness-copy neutral-B end animation handlers, including ftKb_NsSpecialNEnd_Anim and ftKb_NsSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, with shared setup through a new static inline ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr.
Added ftparts include in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c to support ftPartSetRotX usage during Ness-copy neutral-B end animation cleanup.

Implemented Kirby copy Pr-special start helpers ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101560 and ftKb_SpecialNPr_80101618, selecting right/left ground or air motion states from facing_dir, clearing cmd_vars, resetting animation state, and initializing special move fields from ftKb_DatAttrs.

Implemented ftKb_PrSpecialNStart_Coll to transition Kirby's Pr-special start from ground to air state, preserve current animation frame, and reassign callbacks including death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, deal_dmg_cb, and x21F8.
Added header prototypes in ftKb_Init.h for fn_80100E0C and fn_80105978, converting one prior placeholder declaration into a real prototype and exposing another callback target.
Implemented Kirby Pikachu/Pichu neutral-B collision transition ftKb_PkSpecialN_Coll, choosing Pichu-specific air motion when Kirby's hat kind is FTKIND_PICHU and otherwise using the Pikachu motion.

Implemented Kirby Bowser/Giga Bowser neutral-B ground-to-air collision transitions for start, loop, and end states in ftKb_KpSpecialNStart_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialN_Coll, and ftKb_KpSpecialNEnd_Coll, selecting Giga Koopa motion states when hat kind is FTKIND_GKOOPS.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394, returning an ftLk_SpecialNIndex for Kirby Link/Young Link copied neutral-B states by subtracting the appropriate base motion ID when the current motion is one of the copied Link or Clink neutral-B start/loop/end ground/air states.

Implemented Kirby Sheik-copy neutral-B end animation handlers ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Anim and ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, including repeated counter cases that set mv/fv flags and air ending behavior that either enters fall or calls ftCo_80096900 based on specialn_sk_freefall_toggle.
Added Nana header prototype ftNn_Init_8012309C so Popo special-hi throw logic can query Nana state.

Implemented Ice Climbers ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter, including velocity scaling by ftIceClimberAttributes x84/x88, calling ftPp_SpecialHi_801218F8, maxing jumps used, clearing command variables, and resetting Popo-specific fields.

Implemented Ice Climbers ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys, applying fall physics attributes x8C/x90 and reading Nana's FtPart_L4thNb joint position via Player_GetEntityAtIndex and lb_8000B1CC when Nana is in a SpecialHi_0 through SpecialHi_5 motion.

Implemented ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Anim, including frame-end fallback to ft_8008A2BC, command-variable gate, Nana readiness check through ftNn_Init_8012309C, and transition into ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C when conditions are met.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C, handling ground versus air state setup, calling ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68, changing to motion state 0x162, and setting x21F8 to ftCommon_8007F76C.

Implemented ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Enter for aerial Ice Climbers down-B, clearing throw flags, cmd_vars, and speciallw fields, changing to ftPp_MS_SpecialAirLw, starting animation command processing, and assigning accessory4_cb.
Refined ftIceClimberAttributes in ftPopo/types.h by replacing padding at 0x88-0x94 with named float fields x88, x8C, and x90 used by the new special-hi air entry and physics code.
smart_moves: Factored repeated Ness-copy neutral-B attribute initialization into static inline ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr, avoiding duplicated writes to mv.kb.specialn_ns fields and ns_flash_gobj.
Preserved animation continuity during ground-to-air collision transitions by passing fp->cur_anim_frame into Fighter_ChangeMotionState for Kirby copy collision handlers.
Used Kirby hat kind switches to select equivalent copied-character variants, such as Pichu versus Pikachu, Giga Koopa versus Koopa, and Link versus Clink motion-state families.
Matched callback side effects during state transitions, notably restoring Kirby Pr-special callbacks after ftKb_PrSpecialNStart_Coll changes motion state.
Promoted Ice Climber attribute padding into explicit float fields only where the decompiled code needed them, keeping the struct change minimal and evidence-driven.
For Ice Climbers up-B, checked Nana's entity and motion range before reading her joint transform, preventing blind assumptions about the partner object.
decomp_lessons: Kirby copy-special decomp often needs hat-kind-dependent motion ID selection; matching code should preserve the original switch shape and default behavior rather than collapsing all variants.
Collision handlers that convert ground special states into air special states commonly call ftCommon_8007D5D4, reuse cur_anim_frame, and pass specific transition flags to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Freefall toggles in fighter dat attributes can decide between ftCo_Fall_Enter and ftCo_80096900; do not assume all aerial special end states enter plain fall.
Objdiff matches may require explicit stack padding such as PAD_STACK or unused local u8 arrays; these are matching artifacts and should be retained when needed.
When decompiling partner-character logic like Ice Climbers, inspect Player_GetEntityAtIndex interactions and partner motion ranges instead of treating the move as single-fighter-only.
Struct field recovery should be driven by code usage: ftIceClimberAttributes x88, x8C, and x90 became floats because the decompiled functions divide velocities and pass them to fall physics.
Header prototypes should be added or converted from placeholder comments as soon as decompiled functions are referenced as callbacks or cross-file helpers.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied special files use ftKb_SpecialNxx.c names, with copied-character abbreviations such as Ns for Ness, Pk for Pikachu/Pichu, Kp for Koopa/Bowser, Lk for Link, Cl for Young Link/Clink, Sk for Sheik, and Zd in the file name shown for the broader special group.

Motion IDs for Kirby copied specials follow ftKb_MS_<copy><Special/AirSpecial>N<phase> patterns, for example ftKb_MS_PkSpecialAirN, ftKb_MS_KpSpecialAirNStart, ftKb_MS_GkSpecialAirNEnd, ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNStartR, and ftKb_MS_PrSpecialAirNStartL.
Unknown but address-anchored functions remain named fn_80xxxxxx or ftKb_SpecialNPr_80xxxxxx when semantics are not yet confidently known.
Ice Climbers code keeps Popo-prefixed functions in ftPp_SpecialS.c and uses Nana-prefixed initialization/query prototypes such as ftNn_Init_8012309C when checking the partner fighter.
Recovered attribute fields in ftPopo/types.h were named x88, x8C, and x90 rather than speculative semantic names, reflecting weak semantic evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used small unused stack locals such as u8 _[8], u8 _[16], and PAD_STACK(...) to satisfy stack layout for matching.
Retained repeated GET_FIGHTER calls and scoped temporaries in several functions, likely to reproduce compiler register allocation and instruction ordering.
Used direct floating comparisons in the same style as the source match, such as 1.0f == fp->mv.pr.specialn.x34.x and da->specialn_ns_freefall_toggle == 0.0f.
Kept explicit command variable clearing chains, for example fp->cmd_vars[0] = fp->cmd_vars[1] = fp->cmd_vars[2] = fp->cmd_vars[3] = 0, to match generated code.
Preserved specific Fighter_ChangeMotionState flag constants such as 0x0C4C5082, 0x0C4C5080, 0x0C4C5880, and 0x0C4C5092U rather than replacing them with guessed symbolic names.
Computed Link-copy neutral-B indices by subtracting the base motion ID from the current motion ID, matching the compact assembly pattern for contiguous motion-state ranges.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The PR body reports verification by objdiff at 100% for all functions and main.dol SHA1 OK.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftIceClimberAttributes x88, x8C, or x90 to semantic field names.
Search for follow-up decompilation of fn_80105978 in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c.
Search for follow-up decompilation or naming of fn_80100E0C and ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C.
Compare other Kirby copy-special collision handlers for the same ground-to-air transition flag patterns.
Find future review discussion about replacing raw Fighter_ChangeMotionState constants in Kirby or Ice Climbers special move code.
